word: H885 - בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי־יַעֲקַן - Bᵉʼêrôth Bᵉnêy-Yaʻăqan - be-ay-roth' be-nay' yah-a-can' - Beeroth of the children of Jaakan

sections.paragraph: Short Definition: Beeroth of the children of Jaakan

Original: בּארת בּני־יעקן

Transliteration: be'êrôth benêy-ya‛ăqan

Phonetic: be-ay-roth' be-nay' yah-a-can'

BDB Definition:

Beeroth Bene-Jaakan or Beeroth = "wells" or " wells of the sons of Jaakan"
  1. an encampment for Israel in the wilderness probably in the country of the Horites

Origin: from the fem. pl. of H875, and the pl. contraction of H1121, and H3292

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
